mechanism_narrative: A persistently low FEDFUNDS rate anchors discount rates and supports equity multiples, so when policy stays near zero and does not rise, S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in a majority of low-rate observations, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and slower earnings growth, which should depress equity prices over the next month when unemployment is accelerating.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce non-negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not materially below unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates strengthening final demand and housing-linked activity, which should lift near-term S&P 500 returns as investors revise growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal has no incremental predictive power over either series alone, or if triggered joint-strength periods fail to outperform by at least 1 percentage point on average over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in INDPRO relative to prior trend reflects weakening industrial supply and corporate output, which should weigh on equity returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-average INDPRO episodes do not lead to lower 60-day SPX returns than above-average episodes, or if the sign of the effect reverses in a rolling 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and an unchanged FEDFUNDS rate implies a more negative real rate, which supports risky asset valuations and should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-policy interaction does not outperform a CPI-only or FEDFUNDS-only model, or if triggered periods show flat or negative 20-day SPX returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series indicates payroll contraction and weaker household cash flow, so when employment growth turns negative the S&P 500 should underperform over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average short-horizon return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth both accelerate, the economy is simultaneously expanding in output and spending, creating a broad earnings tailwind that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise versus their prior quarter/month trend
failure_condition: Reject if joint output-spending acceleration does not improve 60-day SPX returns relative to either proxy alone, or if the effect disappears after accounting for market-wide trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in INDPRO and rise in UNRATE indicates a supply-side and labor-market deterioration that should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint industrial-labor stress signal fails to generate lower 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the sign is not stable across rolling 2-year windows.
